key_points: The judiciary is an independent branch of government responsible for interpreting laws and ensuring justice in society
It protects the Constitution and ensures that laws and government actions follow constitutional principles
Judicial independence means that courts can make decisions without interference from the legislature or executive
The Indian judicial system has a hierarchical structure consisting of the Supreme Court, High Courts and District Courts
The judiciary protects Fundamental Rights and provides citizens the power to approach courts if their rights are violated
Judicial review allows courts to examine and declare laws unconstitutional if they violate the Constitution
examples: Citizens can approach the Supreme Court directly when their Fundamental Rights are violated
High Courts hear appeals from district courts and supervise lower courts within the state
Courts can strike down laws that are inconsistent with the Constitution
Judges interpret laws to resolve disputes between citizens, organizations or governments
formulas: Judiciary → Justice + Interpretation of Law + Protection of Constitution
Independent Judiciary → No interference + Fair decisions
Judicial Review → Court checks law + Constitution
exam_tips: Explain the concept of an independent judiciary clearly
Mention the hierarchy of courts in India
Write about the role of the judiciary in protecting Fundamental Rights
Include terms like judicial review and constitutional protection in answers
